prompt: Who holds the record for the fastest recorded serve speed in men's tennis history?
explanation: Australia's Sam Groth recorded a serve of 263 km/h (163.4 mph) in 2012, the fastest officially recorded.

prompt: Who holds the record for the fastest recorded serve speed in women's tennis history?
explanation: Sabine Lisicki hit a serve of 210.8 km/h (131 mph) in 2014, a WTA record.

prompt: Which player has the most career ATP singles titles in the Open Era?
explanation: Jimmy Connors won a record 109 ATP singles titles during his career.

prompt: Which player holds the record for most consecutive tour-level match wins in the Open Era (men's)?
explanation: Guillermo Vilas holds the men's record with a 46-match winning streak in 1977, though some records from that era are disputed.

prompt: Which women's player holds the longest winning streak in the Open Era, with 74 consecutive wins in 1984?
explanation: Martina Navratilova's 74-match winning streak in 1984 is the longest in the Open Era for either tour.

prompt: Which player is the youngest to win a Grand Slam singles title in the Open Era (women's)?
explanation: Martina Hingis won the 1997 Australian Open at 16 years and 3 months old.

prompt: Which player is the oldest man to be ranked world No.1 in ATP history?
explanation: Djokovic held the world No.1 ranking at age 36 in 2023, the oldest man ever to do so.

prompt: Which player was the youngest ever to be ranked ATP world No.1?
explanation: Carlos Alcaraz became world No.1 at 19 years old in September 2022, the youngest ever.

prompt: Which player, before Alcaraz, held the record as the youngest ATP world No.1?
explanation: Lleyton Hewitt became world No.1 at age 20 in 2001, a record until Alcaraz surpassed it in 2022.

prompt: Which player won the 1999 French Open at 33 years old, making her the oldest woman to win a Grand Slam in the professional era at that time?
explanation: Steffi Graf's 1999 French Open win came in the twilight of her career, shortly before her retirement.

prompt: Which player holds the record for most Grand Slam mixed doubles titles?
explanation: Margaret Court holds records across singles, doubles and mixed doubles combined at Grand Slams.

prompt: Which player has won the most career Grand Slam titles when combining singles, doubles, and mixed doubles?
explanation: Martina Navratilova holds the record with 59 total Grand Slam titles across all disciplines.

prompt: Which player recorded the fastest ever Grand Slam match win, defeating an opponent in just 34 minutes at the 1988 French Open?
options: Natasha Zvereva was on the losing end of this match
explanation: Steffi Graf beat Natasha Zvereva 6-0, 6-0 in just 32 minutes in the 1988 French Open final, one of the most lopsided major finals ever.

prompt: What was the score of the extremely lopsided 1988 French Open women's final won by Steffi Graf?
explanation: Graf demolished Natasha Zvereva 6-0, 6-0 in one of the most one-sided Grand Slam finals ever.

prompt: Which player is the only one in tennis history to win a singles 'Golden Slam' — all four majors plus Olympic gold in the same calendar year?
explanation: Steffi Graf achieved the singles Golden Slam in 1988, a feat still unmatched by any other player.

prompt: What term is used for Serena Williams's feat of holding all four Grand Slam titles simultaneously (though not in the same calendar year) on two separate occasions?
explanation: Serena Williams achieved the 'Serena Slam' twice, in 2002-03 and 2014-15.

prompt: Which player has the most career prize money earnings in tennis history among men?
explanation: Novak Djokovic has earned more prize money than any other tennis player in history.

prompt: Which player is the all-time leading prize money earner among women in tennis history?
explanation: Serena Williams has earned more career prize money than any other female tennis player.

prompt: Which player is known for having the most career aces served in tennis history (men's tour)?
explanation: Ivo Karlovic served more career aces than any other player in ATP history.

prompt: Which tall Croatian player, standing 6'11, holds the ATP career aces record?
explanation: Ivo Karlovic's height and serve made him one of the most dominant servers in tennis history.

prompt: Which player famously won Wimbledon as a wildcard entrant in 2001?
explanation: Goran Ivanisevic became the only wildcard to ever win Wimbledon, beating Pat Rafter in the final.

prompt: Which player lost three previous Wimbledon finals before finally winning as a wildcard in 2001?
explanation: Ivanisevic had lost Wimbledon finals in 1992, 1994, and 1998 before his triumphant 2001 win.

prompt: Which player holds the record for most Wimbledon men's singles titles won within a single decade, with five between 1976 and 1980?
explanation: Bjorn Borg's five Wimbledon titles from 1976-1980 is the most won by any man within a single decade.

prompt: Which player won three consecutive Wimbledon titles as a teenager in the 1980s?
explanation: Boris Becker won Wimbledon in 1985 (age 17) and 1986, becoming a global star as a teenager.

prompt: Which player became the youngest ever Wimbledon men's singles champion, at 17 years old in 1985?
explanation: Boris Becker remains the youngest man to win Wimbledon, at 17 years and 227 days old.

prompt: Which player, at 17 years old, won the 1989 French Open, becoming the youngest Grand Slam men's champion in the modern era?
explanation: Michael Chang won the 1989 French Open at just 17 years old, still the youngest male Grand Slam champion of the Open Era.

prompt: Which unusual tactic did Michael Chang use during his marathon fourth-round match against Ivan Lendl at the 1989 French Open?
options: Underarm serves to disrupt Lendl's rhythm
explanation: Chang used underhand serves in the fifth set to combat cramping and disrupt Lendl, ultimately winning the match.

prompt: Which player has the record for most career clay-court titles in ATP history?
explanation: Rafael Nadal holds the record for career clay-court titles, reflecting his dominance on the surface.

prompt: What is Rafael Nadal's win-loss record at the French Open across his career, widely cited as one of the most dominant records in sports?
explanation: Nadal finished his French Open career with a remarkable 112-4 win-loss record.

prompt: Which player defeated Nadal at the French Open in 2009, ending his 31-match unbeaten streak at the tournament?
explanation: Robin Soderling shocked Nadal in the fourth round of the 2009 French Open, his first ever loss at Roland Garros.

prompt: Which two other players, besides Robin Soderling, have beaten Nadal at the French Open?
options: Stan Wawrinka and Rafael Nadal himself
explanation: Djokovic beat Nadal at the French Open in 2015 and 2021, and Zverev beat him in 2024, joining Soderling as the only players to do so.

prompt: Which player has won the most Grand Slam mixed doubles titles among active or recently active players, also a men's doubles specialist?
explanation: Leander Paes has won numerous Grand Slam mixed doubles titles across a long career.

prompt: Which player retired with the most career singles match wins in the Open Era among men?
explanation: Jimmy Connors retired with 1,274 career match wins, a men's Open Era record.

statement: Sam Groth holds the record for the fastest officially recorded serve in men's tennis.
explanation: Groth's 263 km/h serve in 2012 remains the fastest ever officially recorded.

explanation: Hingis won the 1997 Australian Open at just 16 years old.

explanation: Alcaraz reached world No.1 at 19 years old in September 2022, surpassing Lleyton Hewitt's previous record.

explanation: Becker won his first Wimbledon title in 1985 at 17 years old.

prompt: Order these tennis youngest-champion records chronologically by when they were set (earliest first).
events: Boris Becker becomes the youngest Wimbledon men's champion (1985)
Michael Chang becomes the youngest French Open men's champion of the Open Era (1989)
Martina Hingis becomes the youngest Open Era Grand Slam women's champion (1997)
Carlos Alcaraz becomes the youngest ATP world No.1 (2022)
explanation: Order: Becker 1985, Chang 1989, Hingis 1997, Alcaraz 2022.

prompt: Which player's 2009 French Open loss to Robin Soderling is considered one of the biggest upsets in tennis history?
explanation: Nadal's shock fourth-round loss ended his 31-match unbeaten run at Roland Garros.

prompt: Which player benefited most directly from Nadal's shock 2009 French Open loss, going on to win the title himself?
explanation: With Nadal out, Federer won the 2009 French Open, completing his career Grand Slam.

prompt: Which achievement did Roger Federer complete by winning the 2009 French Open?
explanation: The 2009 French Open was the only major missing from Federer's collection, completing his career Grand Slam.

prompt: Which of these top players reached a Grand Slam singles final but never won one during his career?
explanation: Ferrer, Nishikori and Berdych all reached exactly one Grand Slam singles final each without ever winning a major.

prompt: Which player was ranked world No.1 for a period without ever winning a Grand Slam singles title, a rare feat?
explanation: Marcelo Rios reached world No.1 in 1998 without ever winning a Grand Slam singles title, a unique case in ATP history.

prompt: Which country is Marcelo Rios from?
explanation: Marcelo Rios, from Chile, remains the only man to reach world No.1 without a Grand Slam title.

prompt: Which player has the most career doubles Grand Slam titles among men, playing primarily with his twin brother?
options: Both Bryan brothers share the joint record, having always played together
explanation: Bob and Mike Bryan share every one of their record Grand Slam doubles titles, having played exclusively together.

prompt: Which unusual injury forced Rafael Nadal to retire mid-match from the 2018 Australian Open semi-final?
explanation: Nadal retired due to a leg/groin injury during his semi-final against Marin Cilic in 2018.

prompt: Which chronic condition has Rafael Nadal managed throughout much of his career, affecting his foot?
explanation: Nadal has managed Mueller-Weiss syndrome, a rare foot condition, for much of his career.

prompt: Which player won an Olympic singles gold medal and later a doubles gold medal partnering with his brother?
options: Rafael Nadal (singles 2008, doubles with Marc Lopez 2016)
explanation: Nadal won singles gold in Beijing 2008 and doubles gold with Marc Lopez in Rio 2016 (not his brother, but a close friend and compatriot).

prompt: Which two players won the men's doubles gold medal at the 2012 London Olympics, both former singles Wimbledon champions from the same country?
options: The Bryan brothers won gold, from the USA
explanation: Bob and Mike Bryan won Olympic doubles gold for the USA at the London 2012 Games.

prompt: Which player won the singles silver medal at the 2012 London Olympics, losing the final to Andy Murray on home soil?
explanation: Federer lost the Olympic final to Murray at Wimbledon's Centre Court in 2012.

prompt: Which player won bronze at the 2012 London Olympics after a marathon semi-final loss to Federer?
explanation: Del Potro beat Djokovic for bronze after a grueling semi-final loss to Federer that lasted over four hours.

prompt: Which Argentine player beat both Federer and Nadal en route to winning the 2009 US Open?
explanation: Del Potro beat Nadal in the semi-final and Federer in the final to win his only Grand Slam title.

prompt: Which score did Juan Martin del Potro win the deciding fifth set by against Federer in the 2009 US Open final?
explanation: Del Potro won the fifth set 6-2, in a match remembered for his power hitting against Federer's finesse.

prompt: Which player is nicknamed the 'Swiss Express' or associated with an era-defining rivalry called 'Fedal' with Nadal?
explanation: The Federer-Nadal rivalry, nicknamed 'Fedal', is one of the most celebrated in tennis history.

prompt: How many times did Federer and Nadal meet in tour-level matches throughout their careers?
explanation: Federer and Nadal played 40 times across their careers, with Nadal leading the head-to-head.

prompt: Who led the career head-to-head record between Federer and Nadal?
explanation: Nadal led the head-to-head 24-16 across their storied rivalry.

prompt: Which player has the highest winning percentage on grass courts in the Open Era among men with significant match totals?
explanation: Federer's grass-court winning percentage remains among the highest of the Open Era.

prompt: Which surface has Rafael Nadal's win percentage historically been highest on?
explanation: Nadal's win percentage on clay is significantly higher than on any other surface, reflecting his dominance there.

prompt: Which player is credited with popularizing the two-handed backhand, which became standard among later generations?
options: Chris Evert and Bjorn Borg were early prominent users
explanation: Chris Evert and Bjorn Borg were among the first prominent players to popularize the two-handed backhand in the 1970s.

prompt: Which women's player famously used a two-handed backhand and dominated the 1970s alongside Martina Navratilova?
explanation: Chris Evert's rivalry with Martina Navratilova was one of the defining storylines of 1970s and 80s women's tennis.

prompt: How many times did Chris Evert and Martina Navratilova play each other over their careers, one of the greatest rivalries in tennis?
explanation: Evert and Navratilova met 80 times across their careers, an extraordinary rivalry by any sport's standards.

prompt: Who led the head-to-head record between Chris Evert and Martina Navratilova?
explanation: Navratilova led their rivalry 43-37 across their many meetings.

prompt: Which historic tennis match, dubbed the 'Battle of the Sexes', saw Billie Jean King beat Bobby Riggs in 1973?
options: Billie Jean King defeated Bobby Riggs in straight sets
The match ended in a draw
explanation: King's straight-sets win over the 55-year-old Riggs was a landmark moment for women's sports.

prompt: Which organization did Billie Jean King help found to support women's professional tennis in 1970?
explanation: King was instrumental in founding the Virginia Slims Circuit, a precursor to the WTA, established in 1973.

prompt: In what year was the WTA (Women's Tennis Association) founded, with Billie Jean King as a key figure?
explanation: The WTA was founded in 1973, largely through the efforts of Billie Jean King and other leading players.

prompt: Which player achieved the rare feat of winning the singles, doubles and mixed doubles titles at the same Grand Slam (a 'Boodle Slam' or triple)?
options: Margaret Court accomplished this multiple times
explanation: Margaret Court won the singles, doubles, and mixed doubles 'triple' at several Grand Slams during her career.

prompt: How many Grand Slam singles titles did Margaret Court win, an all-time record across the amateur and Open eras combined?
explanation: Margaret Court's 24 Grand Slam singles titles is the most in tennis history, spanning both amateur and Open eras.

prompt: Which player won a men's singles Grand Slam without dropping a single set throughout the entire tournament in the modern Open Era, a rare feat?
options: Multiple players have achieved this, including Djokovic (2023 Wimbledon) and Nadal at times
No player has ever done this
explanation: Several top players have won majors without dropping a set, a mark of complete dominance through a tournament.

prompt: Which player completed 'Sunshine Double' wins (Indian Wells and Miami in the same year) most often in ATP history?
explanation: Djokovic has won the 'Sunshine Double' (Indian Wells and Miami back-to-back) more times than any other player.

prompt: Which term refers to winning both the Indian Wells Masters and Miami Open in the same season?
explanation: The 'Sunshine Double' refers to winning both major hard-court events held in the United States each spring.

statement: Marcelo Rios reached world No.1 in the ATP rankings without ever winning a Grand Slam singles title.
explanation: Rios remains the only man to achieve the world No.1 ranking without a major title.

explanation: Del Potro's run included beating Nadal in the semi-final and Federer in the final.

explanation: Nadal led their storied rivalry 24-16 in tour-level matches.

statement: Billie Jean King beat Bobby Riggs in the famous 1973 'Battle of the Sexes' match.
explanation: King's win was a milestone moment in the fight for gender equality in sports.

statement: Margaret Court holds the record for most Grand Slam singles titles in tennis history, with 24.
explanation: Court's 24 titles span both the amateur and Open eras, a total unmatched by any other player.

statement: Chris Evert and Martina Navratilova played each other 80 times across their careers.
explanation: Their rivalry is one of the most extensive in tennis history, with Navratilova leading 43-37.

statement: The Bryan brothers, Bob and Mike, hold the men's doubles Grand Slam titles record, having played almost exclusively together.
explanation: The twin brothers built their entire doubles careers as a partnership.

statement: Rafael Nadal has managed a rare foot condition called Mueller-Weiss syndrome throughout much of his career.
explanation: This chronic condition affected Nadal's playing schedule at various points in his career.

statement: Novak Djokovic has completed the 'Sunshine Double' more times than any other player.
explanation: Djokovic has repeatedly won both Indian Wells and Miami in the same season.

prompt: Which player's rare achievement of winning a Grand Slam without losing a set came at the 2017 French Open?
explanation: Nadal won the 2017 French Open without dropping a single set throughout the tournament.

prompt: Which women's player won the 2017 Australian Open without dropping a set, part of her career Grand Slam collection?
explanation: Serena Williams beat her sister Venus in the final to win the 2017 Australian Open, one of her 23 major titles.

statement: Rafael Nadal won the 2017 French Open without dropping a single set.
explanation: Nadal's dominant run through the 2017 tournament included no sets lost.

statement: Serena Williams beat her sister Venus in the final of the 2017 Australian Open.
